display.purpose: Page titles like "Board". The loudest type on any screen.

title.purpose: Panel and section headings, empty-state titles.

body.purpose: Card titles, descriptions, the working text of the app.

shadows.name: rest
shadows.purpose: Every card and panel at rest, over a hairline border.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Interactive card on hover, paired with a 2px upward translate.

shadows.name: float
shadows.purpose: Dialogs and overlays only. Never on inline content.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Ease-out-expo for entrances and lifts; no bounce.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Default transition for color, border, and transform on buttons and links.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single loudest action on a screen. Ink fill, pill shape.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; border: none; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); font-family: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 180ms ease, transform 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--primary) 90%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in oklab, var(--ring) 40%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0.5px); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Everything that is not the one primary action. Mist fill, hairline border, ink text.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-secondary"><svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><circle cx="8" cy="8" r="2.5"/><path d="M8 1.5v2M8 12.5v2M1.5 8h2M12.5 8h2"/></svg>Agents</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--muted); color: var(--foreground); font-family: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--card); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in oklab, var(--ring) 40%, transparent); }

components.name: Project Chip
components.description: Project selector pill. Active = ink border + ink-tinted fill; inactive = mist.
components.css: .ds-chip { padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid var(--border); background: var(--muted); color: var(--muted-foreground); font-family: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: color 180ms ease, background-color 180ms ease; } .ds-chip:hover { color: var(--foreground); } .ds-chip-active { border-color: var(--primary); background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--primary) 10%, transparent); color: var(--primary); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: The only place saturated status color is allowed. Tint fill 15%, border 30%, full-strength text.
components.html: ApprovedChanges requestedvisual_review
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid; font-family: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.75rem; } .ds-badge-neutral { background: var(--muted); color: var(--muted-foreground); border-color: var(--border); } .ds-badge-success { background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--success) 15%, transparent); color: var(--success); border-color: color-mix(in oklab, var(--success) 30%, transparent); } .ds-badge-warning { background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--warning) 15%, transparent); color: var(--warning); border-color: color-mix(in oklab, var(--warning) 30%, transparent); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Pill field, mist fill, hairline stroke. Border shifts to ink on focus, soft ring, no glow.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid var(--input); background: var(--muted); color: var(--foreground); font-family: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in oklab, var(--ring) 30%, transparent); }

components.name: Task Card
components.description: Leaf surface on the board. Flat at rest, lifts on hover. The ink pulse dot marks a task waiting on you.
components.html:
Review the new pricing screenshot

components.css: .ds-card { border-radius: 16px; border: 1px solid var(--border); background: var(--card); padding: 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.05); cursor: pointer; transition: transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 200ms ease; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.07); } .ds-card:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in oklab, var(--ring) 40%, transparent); } .ds-card-head { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; justify-content: space-between; gap: 8px; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-family: Manrope, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.35; color: var(--foreground); } .ds-pulse { margin-top: 4px; flex-shrink: 0; width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--primary); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { .ds-pulse { animation: ds-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite; } } @keyframes ds-pulse { 50% { opacity: 0.4; } } .ds-card-tags { margin-top: 8px; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 6px; } .ds-card-meta { margin: 8px 0 0; font-size: 0.75rem; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: var(--muted-foreground); }

narrative.overview: loophand is the calm operator's panel between an agent and the person who has to say yes. The agent is blocked; the human is busy; the only job of the surface is to route that one decision and get out of the way. The board is ink on paper, near-silent, and the single card you are acting on is the only thing that earns weight. The aesthetic is the Linear / Raycast lane: monochrome, precise, confident, legible at a glance. Hierarchy comes from weight and size, never decoration. Color is rationed to status alone. Surfaces are flat until you touch them.
keyCharacteristics: Two-tone by default: ink on paper, color only for status.
One typeface (Manrope) carries the entire hierarchy.
Flat at rest; elevation is a response to interaction, never decoration.
Pill geometry for anything actionable; 16-24px radii for content surfaces.
Light and dark are equal first-class themes, never a reskin.

rules.name: The Ink-and-Paper Rule
rules.body: The interface is two-tone. Ink on paper, nothing else, until a state needs to speak. If you are reaching for a colored fill to make something look nicer, the answer is weight, size, or space, not hue.

rules.name: The Status-Is-Earned Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, and red are reserved for task state and outcomes. They are forbidden as decorative accents, brand color, or emphasis. A color on screen always means 'this is what happened to a task.'

rules.name: The One-Voice Rule
rules.body: A single typeface carries the entire hierarchy. Contrast is built from weight (400 to 800) and size, never from switching families.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest and separated by a hairline. A shadow only appears as a response to state (hover lift, focus, or an overlay). A resting card with a heavy shadow is a bug.

dos: Do keep the board two-tone: ink on paper. Build emphasis from weight (400-800) and size, not color.
Do reserve green / amber / red for task state and outcomes only, rendered as a tinted badge (15% fill, 30% border, full-strength text).
Do keep surfaces flat at rest (shadow-sm + hairline) and let shadow appear only on hover-lift or true overlays.
Do use pill geometry for anything actionable and 16-24px radii for anything that holds content.
Do carry status with a label or shape in addition to any color, so it survives color blindness and a one-second glance.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion on every new transition.
Do keep both themes in parity: design the token, not the light-mode screen.
donts: Don't ship generic SaaS slop: no gradient hero-metric cards, no identical icon-card grids, no purple gradients, no AI-template look.
Don't drift toward heavy enterprise: no Jira / ServiceNow density, cluttered toolbars, or nested panels.
Don't use crypto / neon: no neon-on-black, no glow effects, no hype aesthetic.
Don't go cutesy / toy-like: no over-rounding into a toy, no emoji-as-UI, no mascots.
Don't use a colored border-left / border-right stripe as an accent. Use a full hairline, a tint, or a leading dot.
Don't put background-clip: text gradients on type. One solid ink color; emphasis via weight or size.
Don't add a second typeface to create hierarchy. Reach for a heavier Manrope weight first.
Don't rest a card under a heavy shadow or nest a card inside a card.
Don't reach for a modal as the first answer. Exhaust inline and progressive surfaces.
